book_claims.claim: Pasta should be consumed in limited portions as it is easily converted to sugars; paired with vegetables, legumes, and olive oil to lower glycemic load
book_claims.mechanism: Refined semolina pasta has moderate-to-high glycemic index; rapidly converted to glucose in the intestine

book_claims.claim: Primary grain/starch in many Longevity Diet dinners; whole-grain pasta provides folate (83 mcg per ½ cup enriched cooked, 21% DV) and iron (1 mg per cup whole wheat cooked, 6% DV)
book_claims.mechanism: Complex carbohydrate source; enriched/whole-wheat varieties provide folate and iron

supplementary_research.finding: Pasta consumption within a Mediterranean diet not associated with increased BMI or cardiometabolic risk; glycemic impact reduced when eaten with olive oil and vegetables
Cooling cooked pasta increases resistant starch content, lowering glycemic response by up to 50%; relevant to pasta dishes served at room temperature

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: Enriched pasta is a reliable folate source; bioavailability ~85%
Lower GI than bread; cooling increases resistant starch
Non-heme iron; enhanced absorption with vitamin C-containing vegetables

synergies.description: Pasta e vaianeia — traditional Molochio longevity dish; legumes add protein and fiber, completing amino acid profile
Fat lowers glycemic response and provides healthy MUFA; core Longevity Diet combination
Vegetables add fiber and micronutrients; book explicitly states bulk should shift from pasta to vegetables
